candidateFor: International patients seeking wrinkle reduction at 40-70% lower cost than US or UK clinics
Adults with dynamic wrinkles on forehead, between brows, or around eyes who want gradual smoothing
Patients combining Botox with other aesthetic procedures during a single Korea trip
Those seeking jaw-slimming effects (masseter treatment) as part of facial contouring
Individuals with 3-4 month availability between touch-ups who can plan around treatment timing
Patients wanting minimal downtime during vacation or business travel to Korea

whatToExpect.detail: Your practitioner evaluates facial anatomy, discusses target areas (forehead, glabella, crow's feet, or jaw), reviews your aesthetic goals, and confirms candidacy. Topical numbing cream is applied 20-30 minutes before injection. Photo documentation occurs for before-and-after tracking.

Botulinum toxin is injected into specific muscles using fine needles. No hospitalization needed. Discomfort is minimal with numbing cream. You can walk out and resume light activities immediately. Avoid strenuous exercise, saunas, and alcohol for 24 hours.

whatToExpect.duration: 2-3 weeks settle-in period; plan 3-5 days in Korea post-treatment
whatToExpect.detail: Results appear within 3-5 days and fully settle over 2 weeks. Minor redness or swelling typically fades within hours. Schedule follow-up consultations at 2 weeks if adjustments are needed. Effects last 3-4 months, after which you can retreat. Many international patients book procedures mid-trip to enjoy results throughout their stay.

whyKorea: Cost: Botox injections range USD 80-150 per area—approximately 40-70% less than comparable US or UK clinics with equivalent practitioner experience
Surgeon expertise: Korean aesthetic clinics train extensively in facial anatomy and Asian facial proportions; many practitioners are board-certified and MFDS-regulated
Precision protocols: High-volume clinics have refined injection techniques over decades, with detailed photo documentation and long-term follow-up standards
Technology access: Clinics use authentic, MFDS-approved botulinum toxin products with full supply chain transparency—no counterfeit risk

faqs.question: Will I look frozen after Botox in Korea?
faqs.answer: No. Korean practitioners typically favor natural results that soften expression lines without eliminating movement. Discuss your aesthetic goals during consultation; experienced clinics adjust dosage and placement to preserve your facial character while smoothing target areas.

faqs.question: How long does Botox actually last?
faqs.answer: Effects typically last 3-4 months. Jaw-slimming Botox may persist slightly longer due to different muscle mass. Most patients schedule retreatment every 12-16 weeks to maintain results. Metabolism varies—some individuals metabolize faster or slower than average.

faqs.question: Can I combine Botox with other procedures during my Korea trip?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many international patients pair Botox with dermal fillers, laser treatments, or micro-needling in the same visit. Your practitioner will advise on spacing if combining injectable procedures; typically 2-3 weeks between different injection sites allows adequate healing and assessment.

faqs.question: What happens if I'm not happy with the results?
faqs.answer: Results fully settle at 2 weeks. If you desire adjustment, clinics offer follow-up consultations—sometimes included in your initial package. Botox effects naturally fade over time, so any dissatisfaction resolves within 3-4 months without intervention. Reversal is not standard practice.

faqs.question: Is Botox safe as a medical tourist in Korea?
faqs.answer: Botox is approved by MFDS (Korea's medical regulator) and is administered by licensed practitioners in accredited clinics. Choose established facilities with transparent credentials, real before-and-after documentation, and clear aftercare instructions. Allergic reactions are rare but possible; disclose all medical history upfront.
